# Transcrição: 1-09Positioning

- **Fonte:** 1-09Positioning
- **Duração:** 07:38
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

So this lesson is all about positioning and positioning is the place your product or your brand or even yourself occupies in the mind of your prospect. And I like to think of positioning as well this is a pyramid low, medium and high. And if we think about it say like with car brands Chevy is probably down here at the lower end. It's a lower price, it's a great car but it's not super luxurious, it's not super high end, it's lower priced. There are some that are more low priced but that's just a good one right there. Then maybe the middle would be BMW. Fancy your car, a lot more expensive, a lot more prestige. And then maybe up at the top we might have Rolls Royce or Ferrari or Bentley. Super high end. And this is all about the perception. Positioning is all about perception. Now in our types of businesses for most of us we want to compete on value and be at the high end. We want high end positioning. And I like to think about it up here, you're competing on value, down here you're competing on price. Now again it's all perception because if you, it's perception and it's needs and who you're actually speaking to, who your avatar is. So if there's like a soccer mom who wants to transport her kids and all her kids friends to whatever, to the movie theater then a Ferrari is probably not going to have the highest value, the highest utility for her. Something like a Chevy van or a Chevy SUV would. So it's all about perception and it's about who you're reaching and what's important to them. But for most of us, for the most people going through PLF or many of us in PLF in this world we might be in an expert type of business where we are the expert or we, well we want expert positioning, we want leadership positioning, we want to be seen as a thought leader. Maybe even as a guru, I know a lot of people don't like that word but it's out there and it's used quite a bit. And so what's my positioning? My positioning is as the one of the highest level marketing trainers for online business building right? That's my positioning. I am the absolute number one top leader in launches. There's no one even close. So I'm up at the top end of the market. Now an interesting thing, since we have cars up here, Toyota. So Toyota makes great cars, super durable, on and on and on. They decided they wanted to go at the high end of the market. So Toyota started down here competing with Chevy right? Toyota.

## [03:01 - 06:03]

When they decided they wanted to come out with a high end car, they did. But they couldn't come out with a high end Toyota because they had positioning down here. So they came out with a Lexus. Or actually the Lexus would probably be here with the BMW. That's more about where they're at. But they had to create an entirely new brand to come out with a higher end car. Now I think the reality is that Toyota and the Lexus are very, very, very similar cars. They're probably made in the same factory and they're almost identical except for, I'm sure there are differences but they're very, very similar. But Toyota just couldn't come out with a more expensive Toyota. They had to go and create an entirely new brand. If you really think about that, it shows you the power of positioning. That Toyota had this positioning as a value car in the market. And instead of trying to fight that positioning, they wanted the trouble of coming out with an entirely new brand. Now like I said, for most of us, we want to compete on value. We want to be at the high end of the market. You get a lot of benefits from being at the high end of the market. One of the benefits is you get pricing power. Product launch formula. This program is not an inexpensive program. I'm priced at the high end of the market because I have high end positioning. This is a $7 course. When you have higher pricing, it gives you better margin. And better margin is a lot better for your business. I've talked about this other places in the course, but if you want to change the world, there's some people that are like, Jeff, I just want to change the world. I want to be of service. I want to help people. Well, the way you help people is by being in business. And to be in business, you have to make a profit. And when you have a higher prices and better margin, it allows you to spend more money to acquire a customer. And then you can spend a lot more time and energy helping more people. Another thing that positioning does is it attracts partners. It could be affiliate partners, joint venture partners, content partners. It's great if you're seen at the top end of the market. If you are the leader in the market, it's a lot easier to attract partners. Another thing you can do is attract speaking gigs, which, you know, there's a time and a place for that. I don't really speak anymore. I get invited to speak all the time. I turn most of the 99% of those speaking opportunities I turn down just because it doesn't fit with me and my business and my leverage and my lifestyle. But especially when you're starting out or in the middle, you know, it can be great when you've achieved sort of the super high end positioning that I have in a super high leverage that I've got in my business, maybe not as important, but it can still be great to get those invitations. And it can also get you media.

## [06:03 - 07:38]

And you're seen as the leader in the market. And that's really what we're talking about is being seen as the leader or one of the leaders. It gets the attention of the media. And the other thing it does is it gives you this fancy term, which I call deal flow. And what that means is that you'll start to get people coming to you saying, hey, do you want to be partner on this? Do you want to be an advisor on this? Can I give you equity in this business? Do you want this joint venture deal? Do you want this back end deal? There's all kinds of deal flow that comes to you when you are at the top end of the market. So an important thing to remember is everything you do either builds or it degrades your positioning. So you want to think about what you're doing and what it does for your positioning. If you come out with this high end product, say like product launch formula and then I ended up giving this product away as a bonus for something else that would destroy the positioning of product launch formula. I would never do that. I would never discount. I've never in all the years that I've had PLF out, I've never discounted it. Not one single time. Little things, well, that's a big thing. But all the little things that you do within your business and in your publishing and how you present yourself, either it builds or it degrades your positioning. So be at the top end of your market, compete on value, don't compete on price. It will make your life a lot better and your business a lot more successful.
